message:
fix(actor): supervised restart leaves actor in Stopping state (deadlock)

Two bugs in run_supervised_loop:

1. HIGH: After a supervised actor panics and the supervisor decides to
   restart, cell.state was not reset. If the actor had entered Stopping
   state before panicking (e.g., via stop() call + subsequent panic),
   the restarted actor's run_actor_loop saw state == Stopping and
   immediately exited on empty mailbox. The restarted actor was
   effectively dead — it ran on_start, drained buffered messages, ran
   on_stop, and returned. The supervisor saw a normal completion and
   made no further restart attempts.

   Fix: reset cell.state to Created before entering run_actor_loop.

2. MEDIUM: SupervisionDecision::Restart { delay, .. } computed a
   backoff delay via the supervisor's BackoffStrategy, but the delay
   was destructured away with `..` and never applied. All restarts
   happened immediately, defeating backoff configuration.

   Fix: extract the delay and sleep before creating the new actor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
